_README: Letter-grade thresholds for traits, axes, and overall scores. Two gates: (1) absolute score floor — always operative, derived from z-score thresholds via score = 50 + 15·z; (2) cohort percentile rank within the historical pool per positionFamily — operative only when historicalDistributions.json has been backfilled. A grade requires BOTH gates to pass (when percentile data is available). When percentile data is unavailable, the floor gate alone determines the grade.
_dualGateRationale: The floor gate prevents grade inflation in unusually weak classes. The percentile gate prevents grade compression in unusually strong classes. Both together produce era-stable grading: a 'B Shooting Gravity' wing means the same thing across draft years.

_schema.scoreFloor: Minimum 0-100 score to qualify for the grade. Equivalent z-score is shown for reference.
_schema.cohortPercentileMin: Minimum percentile rank within the historical positionFamily pool (0-100, where 100 = top of pool). Effective only when distributions are loaded.
